query: I fine-tuned Llama-3.1-8B for a customer-intent classification task. I have base-model predictions on a 500-row held-out eval set (68% correct) and fine-tune predictions on the same 500 rows (73% correct). I want to know whether the 5-point improvement is real. The labels are per-row binary correct/incorrect. Walk me through the analysis.
expected_behavior: Identifies this as paired-binary and recommends McNemar's test on the discordant pairs (b and c in the 2x2 contingency table) — NOT chi-squared on the marginal accuracies, AND explicitly notes that the concordant pairs do not contribute to the test
Requires reporting an effect size with a 95% confidence interval (odds ratio c/b OR paired-Δaccuracy = (c-b)/n with paired-proportion CI), NOT a bare p-value as the conclusion

Requires a power-check at n=500 with the observed discordance rate (or against a minimum-detectable-effect the user specifies) before issuing a certify / do-not-certify verdict — flags underpowered evals as 'underpowered-inconclusive' rather than as 'no significant difference'
